what_it_does: A standalone HTML page that demonstrates a WORK | PANEL viewer scaffold whose orientation, split ratios, spacing, and panel fill are driven live by a DNA application record and grammar.
observations: Loads vars.css and surface.js and uses a global DNA API
Uses Google Fonts Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono
Renders toggle buttons for orientation, split configuration, panel fill, and density
Reads APP.viewer_layout and GRAM via DNA.api.dna('application') and DNA.api.grammar()
Applies CSS classes portrait/landscape to a flex split container
Sets slot flex-grow from ratios returned by DNA.splitRatio(cfg,{application:APP,grammar:GRAM})
Re-resolves vertical gaps in the WORK slot with DNA.space(bond,{density,grammar:GRAM})
Switches panel fill in place among marks/face/compare without changing the split
For fill=marks it instantiates DNA.control('gold_action',{state,label,components}) for each declared state
Displays a readout of the viewer_layout provenance, reflow rule, config ratio, panel accepts, density multiplier, and law
standards: Treats layout, spacing, ratios, and controls as data resolved from DNA records rather than hardcoded CSS
Live density changes re-resolve every --dna-space-* consumer and bonded-stack gap
Panel fill is a record-driven control strip rendered from application.components.gold_action.states
conventions: CSS custom properties prefixed with --dna-* for geometry and --page/--body/--charcoal etc. for color
Event delegation on button containers using data-* attributes
Async boot() with top-level await against a global DNA object
Inline style flex values and display:none for zero-ratio slots
concerns: Assumes DNA, DNA.api, DNA.space, DNA.splitRatio, DNA.control, DNA.injectSpace, and DNA.mountAtlas exist globally with no guards
No error handling if application or grammar records fail to load
Uses innerHTML for dynamic slot content and button generation
Inline px values injected via style attributes for spacing
Relies on external Google Fonts CDN
Accessibility features missing: no ARIA labels, focus management, or reduced-motion handling
notes: A live proof-of-concept scaffold that wires a DNA application record into an orientation-aware WORK|PANEL viewer with density-responsive spacing and record-driven panel controls.
questions: What is the exact schema of dna/application.json#viewer_layout?
What do DNA.space, DNA.splitRatio, DNA.control, DNA.injectSpace, and DNA.mountAtlas implement?
Where are surface.js and vars.css located and what do they export?
What do the rule references R2, R3, R1b, and rc-2 denote?
How are grammar.scales.space.bonds and dials.density.multipliers structured?
What is the full shape of application.components.gold_action and its states?
Is this file intended as a production viewer or only a layout proof?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comments state it is a minimal proof that dna/application.json#viewer_layout drives live reflow, and the code reads that record to render WORK|PANEL, reflow by orientation, and resolve ratios and spacing from the grammar.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to validate a DNA-driven design substrate where viewer geometry, proportions, spacing, and controls are derived entirely from centralized records rather than authored CSS or markup.
novelty: The explicit use of a DNA API to resolve spacing, split ratios, and control instances from a grammar/application record inside a single runnable HTML scaffold, plus the bonded-stack gap scaling that updates live with density.
contribution: Provides a concrete integration surface that exercises DNA-driven layout reflow, density-responsive spacing, and record-derived control rendering for a WORK|PANEL viewer scaffold.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML viewer scaffold that renders a configurable WORK|PANEL split, using a DNA grammar and application record to drive orientation, ratios, spacing, and panel fills.
intention_for_embedding: To prove and exercise a DNA-driven layout system where viewer configuration, spacing, and controls are resolved live from design tokens and component records.
